clusters.title: Missing worker still prints the default worker log

member_findings.title: Missing worker still prints the default worker log
member_findings.impact: When no process matches the requested worker address, run only logs a warning and then resolves a default log path. A caller can provide any syntactically valid address and receive the tail of the default worker INFO log, even though that address was not associated with the log being printed. In deployments where logs contain tenant identifiers, object keys, or operational details, this leaks unrelated local worker output through an address-scoped command.
member_findings.fix: Fail when the requested worker address cannot be mapped to a specific worker log, or persist and consult an address-to-log mapping created by the worker startup path so stopped-worker logs are only served when the address-to-file association is known.
